title: B001: Implement common types and pure function packages
description:
**Size:** 🏗️ large
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** high

## Summary

Implement the shared library foundation for all CobaltCore operators (CC-0004/S004): 9 reusable type definitions in `internal/common/types/`, plus 4 pure-function packages — `conditions/` for `metav1.Condition` management, `config/` for INI config rendering and merging, `plugins/` for PasteDeploy pipeline and plugin config generation, and `policy/` for oslo.policy YAML rendering and validation. All functions are pure (no Kubernetes API calls) with table-driven unit tests targeting 80%+ coverage.

## Scope

**Included:**
- 9 shared type definitions in `internal/common/types/`: `ImageSpec`, `DatabaseSpec`, `MessagingSpec`, `CacheSpec`, `SecretRefSpec`, `PolicySpec`, `PluginSpec`, `MiddlewareSpec`, `PipelinePosition` — with JSON tags, godoc comments, and doc comments noting mutual exclusivity constraints (no Go-level `Validate()` methods; validation deferred to CRD webhooks per S011)
- `conditions/` package: `SetCondition`, `IsReady`, `GetCondition`, `AllTrue` — wrapping `k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta` helpers
- `config/` package (pure functions only): `RenderINI`, `MergeDefaults`, `InjectSecrets`, `InjectOsloPolicyConfig`
- `plugins/` package: `PipelineSpec` type (package-internal), `RenderPastePipeline`, `RenderPluginConfig`
- `policy/` package (pure functions only): `RenderPolicyYAML`, `MergePolicies`, `ValidatePolicyRules`
- Table-driven unit tests for all 13 functions (80%+ coverage target)
- Godoc and license headers following existing conventions (`// Feature: CC-0004`, SPDX Apache-2.0)

**Excluded:**
- `config/CreateImmutableConfigMap` — Kubernetes-interacting, belongs to S005
- `policy/LoadPolicyFromConfigMap` — Kubernetes-interacting, belongs to S005
- `database/`, `deployment/`, `job/`, `secrets/`, `tls/` packages — Kubernetes-interacting, all S005
- CRD type definitions (`KeystoneSpec`, etc.) — S011
- Kubebuilder validation markers and webhooks — S011/S012
- Integration tests (envtest) — not needed for pure functions
- DeepCopy generation — not needed until CRD types exist (S011)
- Go-level `Validate()` methods on types — validation via CEL markers on CRDs (S011)

## Visualization

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph types["internal/common/types/"]
        IS["ImageSpec"]
        DS["DatabaseSpec"]
        MS["MessagingSpec"]
        CS["CacheSpec"]
        SRS["SecretRefSpec"]
        PS["PolicySpec"]
        PLS["PluginSpec"]
        MWS["MiddlewareSpec"]
        PP["PipelinePosition"]
    end

    subgraph conditions["internal/common/conditions/"]
        SC["SetCondition"]
        IR["IsReady"]
        GC["GetCondition"]
        AT["AllTrue"]
    end

    subgraph config["internal/common/config/"]
        RI["RenderINI"]
        MD["MergeDefaults"]
        ISec["InjectSecrets"]
        IOP["InjectOsloPolicyConfig"]
    end

    subgraph pluginspkg["internal/common/plugins/"]
        PipeSpec["PipelineSpec - internal"]
        RPP["RenderPastePipeline"]
        RPC["RenderPluginConfig"]
    end

    subgraph policypkg["internal/common/policy/"]
        RPY["RenderPolicyYAML"]
        MP["MergePolicies"]
        VPR["ValidatePolicyRules"]
    end

    DS --> SRS
    MS --> SRS
    MWS --> PP

    RPC --> PLS
    RPP --> MWS
    RPP --> PP
    RPP --> PipeSpec

    IOP --> RI

    subgraph consumers["Future Consumers - S005+"]
        CRD["Keystone CRD Types - S011"]
        REC["Reconciler Sub-reconcilers - S013"]
    end

    types --> CRD
    conditions --> REC
    config --> REC
    pluginspkg --> REC
    policypkg --> REC
```

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph pipeline["Config Generation Pipeline - Pure Functions"]
        A["CRD Spec Values"] --> B["MergeDefaults"]
        B --> C["InjectSecrets"]
        C --> D["InjectOsloPolicyConfig"]
        D --> E["RenderINI"]
        E --> F["keystone.conf string"]
    end

    subgraph pastepipeline["Paste Pipeline Rendering"]
        G["Base Pipeline Definition"] --> H["RenderPastePipeline"]
        I["MiddlewareSpec list"] --> H
        H --> J["api-paste.ini string"]
    end

    subgraph policyflow["Policy Rendering"]
        K["Inline Rules"] --> L["MergePolicies"]
        M["External Rules"] --> L
        L --> N["ValidatePolicyRules"]
        N --> O["RenderPolicyYAML"]
        O --> P["policy.yaml string"]
    end
```

## Key Components

- **`internal/common/types/`** — 9 shared struct definitions with JSON tags and godoc. Types use `corev1.LocalObjectReference` for cluster refs. `DatabaseSpec`/`MessagingSpec`/`CacheSpec` document ClusterRef vs. brownfield mutual exclusivity via doc comments (enforcement deferred to CEL markers in S011). `PipelinePosition` is a struct with `After`/`Before` string fields (derived from CRD YAML `position: { after: authtoken }`). `SecretRefSpec` referenced by `DatabaseSpec` and `MessagingSpec`.

- **`internal/common/conditions/`** — 4 functions managing `metav1.Condition` slices, wrapping `k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta`:
  - `SetCondition(conditions *[]metav1.Condition, condition metav1.Condition)` — delegates to `meta.SetStatusCondition`
  - `GetCondition(conditions []metav1.Condition, conditionType string) *metav1.Condition` — delegates to `meta.FindStatusCondition`
  - `IsReady(conditions []metav1.Condition) bool` — checks `type=Ready, status=True`
  - `AllTrue(conditions []metav1.Condition, types ...string) bool` — checks multiple condition types are `True`

- **`internal/common/config/`** — 4 pure-function config rendering operations:
  - `RenderINI(sections map[string]map[string]string) string` — sorted sections, sorted keys within sections
  - `MergeDefaults(userConfig, defaults map[string]map[string]string) map[string]map[string]string` — user values override defaults
  - `InjectSecrets(config map[string]map[string]string, secrets map[string]string) map[string]map[string]string` — replaces placeholder tokens with secret values (e.g., connection strings)
  - `InjectOsloPolicyConfig(config map[string]map[string]string, policyFilePath string)` — mutates config in-place, injects `[oslo_policy] policy_file` when policy overrides are present

- **`internal/common/plugins/`** — Package-internal `PipelineSpec` type with named pipelines (map of pipeline name → ordered filter list), base filter definitions (map of filter name → paste factory + config), and middleware additions from `[]MiddlewareSpec`. `RenderPastePipeline(pipelineSpec PipelineSpec) string` generates complete `api-paste.ini` by inserting middleware at specified positions. `RenderPluginConfig(plugins []PluginSpec) map[string]map[string]string` converts plugin specs into INI section maps.

- **`internal/common/policy/`** — Pure policy operations:
  - `RenderPolicyYAML(rules map[string]string) (string, error)` — serializes rule maps to YAML
  - `MergePolicies(inline, external map[string]string) map[string]string` — inline rules take precedence over external
  - `ValidatePolicyRules(rules map[string]string) field.ErrorList` — returns `field.ErrorList` for empty keys/values

- **Unit tests** — Table-driven tests for all 13 functions using `testing` + Gomega (`g := NewGomegaWithT(t)`) with dot-import convention per existing project patterns (see `bootstrap/manager_test.go`, `builders/secret_builder_test.go`). License headers and `// Feature: CC-0004` tracking. Edge cases: empty inputs, nil maps, sorted output determinism, merge precedence, validation error paths, `PipelinePosition` with both `After`/`Before` fields, middleware insertion at pipeline boundaries.

stories.title: Operator developer uses shared type definitions across operators
stories.want: reusable Go struct definitions (ImageSpec, DatabaseSpec, MessagingSpec, CacheSpec, SecretRefSpec, PolicySpec, PluginSpec, MiddlewareSpec, PipelinePosition) with JSON tags and godoc in internal/common/types/
stories.so_that: all CobaltCore operators share identical type representations, preventing drift and duplication
criteria: All 9 types are defined in internal/common/types/types.go with correct JSON tags
DatabaseSpec, MessagingSpec, CacheSpec document ClusterRef vs brownfield mutual exclusivity via godoc comments
SecretRefSpec is referenced by DatabaseSpec and MessagingSpec
PipelinePosition has After and Before string fields with json tags
Types use corev1.LocalObjectReference for cluster refs
Package has SPDX Apache-2.0 license header and Feature: CC-0004 tracking comment
go vet passes on the types package

stories.title: Operator developer manages status conditions programmatically
stories.want: a conditions/ package with SetCondition, IsReady, GetCondition, and AllTrue functions wrapping k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta
stories.so_that: all operators manage metav1.Condition slices consistently without duplicating apimachinery details
criteria: SetCondition delegates to meta.SetStatusCondition with *[]metav1.Condition parameter
GetCondition delegates to meta.FindStatusCondition and returns *metav1.Condition
IsReady checks for type=Ready with status=True
AllTrue checks multiple condition types are True, returns false if any is missing or not True
All 4 functions have table-driven unit tests with edge cases (empty slices, nil conditions, missing types)

stories.title: Operator developer renders INI config files from Go maps
stories.want: pure config/ package functions (RenderINI, MergeDefaults, InjectSecrets, InjectOsloPolicyConfig) to build OpenStack INI configs without Kubernetes API calls
stories.so_that: the config generation pipeline (CRD spec -> defaults -> secrets -> INI) is testable in isolation
criteria: RenderINI produces deterministic output with sorted sections and sorted keys
MergeDefaults lets user values override defaults while preserving non-overlapping defaults
InjectSecrets replaces placeholder tokens in config values with secret values
InjectOsloPolicyConfig mutates config in-place adding [oslo_policy] policy_file when policy path is non-empty
All 4 functions have table-driven unit tests covering empty inputs, nil maps, and merge precedence

stories.title: Operator developer generates PasteDeploy pipeline configs
stories.want: a plugins/ package with RenderPastePipeline and RenderPluginConfig functions that generate api-paste.ini and plugin INI sections from declarative specs
stories.so_that: OpenStack WSGI pipeline and plugin configuration is generated consistently across operators
criteria: PipelineSpec is package-internal (unexported) with named pipelines, base filter definitions, and middleware additions
RenderPastePipeline generates complete api-paste.ini string with middleware inserted at specified PipelinePosition
RenderPluginConfig converts []PluginSpec into map[string]map[string]string INI sections
Tests cover middleware insertion at pipeline boundaries, empty middleware list, and multiple pipelines

stories.title: Operator developer renders and validates oslo.policy YAML files
stories.want: a policy/ package with RenderPolicyYAML, MergePolicies, and ValidatePolicyRules pure functions
stories.so_that: oslo.policy file rendering, merging, and validation are centralized and testable without Kubernetes
criteria: RenderPolicyYAML serializes map[string]string to YAML string, returns error on marshalling failure
MergePolicies merges inline rules over external rules with inline taking precedence
ValidatePolicyRules returns field.ErrorList for empty keys or empty values
Tests cover empty rule maps, nil inputs, merge precedence, and validation error paths

stories.title: Operator developer relies on comprehensive test coverage for shared library
stories.want: table-driven unit tests for all 13 pure functions achieving 80%+ coverage
stories.so_that: shared library correctness is validated before operators consume it
criteria: All tests use testing + Gomega (g := NewGomegaWithT(t)) with dot-import convention
Tests include edge cases: empty inputs, nil maps, sorted output determinism, merge precedence
Tests include PipelinePosition with both After/Before fields, middleware insertion at boundaries
License headers and Feature: CC-0004 tracking comment present in all test files
go test ./internal/common/... passes with 80%+ coverage for new packages

stories.title: Operator developer finds reference documentation for shared library packages
stories.want: godoc comments on all exported types and functions following existing project conventions
stories.so_that: the shared library is self-documenting and discoverable via IDE and godoc
criteria: Every exported type has a godoc comment explaining its purpose
Every exported function has a godoc comment describing parameters, return values, and behavior
doc.go files exist for each new package with Feature: CC-0004 tracking
SPDX Apache-2.0 license headers on all files

requirements.description: The types/ package SHALL define 9 shared struct types (ImageSpec, DatabaseSpec, MessagingSpec, CacheSpec, SecretRefSpec, PolicySpec, PluginSpec, MiddlewareSpec, PipelinePosition) with JSON struct tags
requirements.rationale: All operators depend on identical type definitions to prevent drift; JSON tags required for CRD serialization

scenarios.name: All types compile and have JSON tags
scenarios.then: go vet passes and all 9 types are defined with json struct tags

scenarios.name: DatabaseSpec references SecretRefSpec
scenarios.then: it contains a SecretRef field of type SecretRefSpec

scenarios.name: PipelinePosition has After and Before fields
scenarios.then: it has After (string, json:"after") and Before (string, json:"before") fields

requirements.description: The types/ package SHALL document ClusterRef vs brownfield mutual exclusivity via godoc comments on DatabaseSpec, MessagingSpec, and CacheSpec
requirements.rationale: Mutual exclusivity is enforced by CEL markers in S011; types must document the constraint for developer guidance

scenarios.name: DatabaseSpec godoc documents mutual exclusivity
scenarios.then: the comment states exactly one of ClusterRef or Host must be set

scenarios.name: MessagingSpec godoc documents mutual exclusivity
scenarios.then: the comment states exactly one of ClusterRef or Hosts must be set

scenarios.name: CacheSpec godoc documents mutual exclusivity
scenarios.then: the comment states exactly one of ClusterRef or Servers must be set

requirements.description: The conditions/ package SHALL provide SetCondition, GetCondition, IsReady, and AllTrue functions wrapping k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta helpers
requirements.rationale: Operators need consistent condition management; wrapping meta helpers prevents direct dependency on apimachinery internals

scenarios.name: SetCondition adds a new condition
scenarios.when: SetCondition is called with a new condition type on an empty slice
scenarios.then: the condition is added to the slice

scenarios.name: SetCondition updates an existing condition
scenarios.when: SetCondition is called with an existing condition type and different status
scenarios.then: the condition is updated in-place

scenarios.name: GetCondition returns nil for missing type
scenarios.when: GetCondition is called with a non-existent condition type
scenarios.then: nil is returned

scenarios.name: IsReady returns true when Ready=True
scenarios.when: IsReady is called on conditions containing Ready=True
scenarios.then: true is returned

scenarios.name: IsReady returns false when Ready is missing
scenarios.when: IsReady is called on empty conditions
scenarios.then: false is returned

scenarios.name: AllTrue returns false when any condition is missing
scenarios.when: AllTrue is called with a type not present in the slice
scenarios.then: false is returned

requirements.description: RenderINI SHALL produce deterministic INI output with sorted sections and sorted keys within each section
requirements.rationale: Deterministic output enables content-hash-based ConfigMap naming; non-deterministic output causes spurious ConfigMap churn

scenarios.name: Sections are sorted alphabetically
scenarios.when: RenderINI is called with sections in non-alphabetical order
scenarios.then: the output has sections in alphabetical order

scenarios.name: Keys within sections are sorted
scenarios.when: RenderINI is called with keys in non-alphabetical order
scenarios.then: keys are sorted within each section

scenarios.name: Empty map produces empty string
scenarios.when: RenderINI is called with an empty map
scenarios.then: empty string is returned

scenarios.name: Deterministic output across calls
scenarios.when: RenderINI is called twice with the same input
scenarios.then: both outputs are byte-identical

requirements.description: MergeDefaults SHALL merge user config over defaults with user values taking precedence
requirements.rationale: CRD spec values must override operator-embedded defaults per config generation pipeline design

scenarios.name: User values override defaults
scenarios.when: both user and defaults have same section/key
scenarios.then: user value is present in the result

scenarios.name: Default values fill gaps
scenarios.when: defaults have keys not in user config
scenarios.then: result contains default values

scenarios.name: Nil user config returns copy of defaults
scenarios.then: result equals defaults

scenarios.name: Nil defaults returns copy of user config
scenarios.then: result equals userConfig

requirements.description: InjectSecrets SHALL replace placeholder tokens in config values with resolved secret values
requirements.rationale: Connection strings contain credentials injected from K8s Secrets at reconciliation time

scenarios.name: Placeholders replaced
scenarios.when: config values contain placeholder tokens matching secrets keys
scenarios.then: placeholders are replaced with secret values

scenarios.name: Non-matching values unchanged
scenarios.when: config values don't contain any placeholders
scenarios.then: values remain unchanged

scenarios.name: Empty secrets map is no-op
scenarios.then: config is returned unchanged

requirements.description: InjectOsloPolicyConfig SHALL inject [oslo_policy] policy_file into config when policyFilePath is non-empty
requirements.rationale: When policyOverrides are configured, the INI config must reference the rendered policy.yaml file

scenarios.name: Policy config injected with non-empty path
scenarios.then: config gains oslo_policy section with policy_file key

scenarios.name: No-op with empty path
scenarios.then: config is not modified

scenarios.name: Existing section preserved
scenarios.when: config already has oslo_policy section with other keys
scenarios.then: policy_file is added without removing existing keys

requirements.description: RenderPastePipeline SHALL generate api-paste.ini string with middleware inserted at specified PipelinePosition
requirements.rationale: OpenStack WSGI pipeline configuration requires correct middleware ordering

scenarios.name: Base pipeline without middleware
scenarios.then: output contains only base pipeline and filter sections

scenarios.name: Middleware after specified filter
scenarios.then: middleware appears after authtoken in pipeline

scenarios.name: Middleware before specified filter
scenarios.then: middleware appears before service_v3 in pipeline

scenarios.name: Empty PipelineSpec
scenarios.when: PipelineSpec is empty (no pipelines, no filters)
scenarios.then: empty string returned

requirements.description: RenderPluginConfig SHALL convert []PluginSpec into INI section map keyed by ConfigSection
requirements.rationale: Plugin config sections must be mergeable into the main config via MergeDefaults

scenarios.name: Plugins produce INI sections
scenarios.then: each PluginSpec produces a section keyed by ConfigSection

scenarios.name: Empty list produces empty map
scenarios.when: plugin list is nil or empty
scenarios.then: empty map is returned

scenarios.name: Multiple plugins produce separate sections
scenarios.when: 2 plugins with different ConfigSections are provided
scenarios.then: result has 2 sections

requirements.description: RenderPolicyYAML SHALL serialize a rule map to valid YAML suitable for oslo.policy
requirements.rationale: The rendered policy.yaml must be parseable by oslo.policy at service startup

scenarios.name: Non-empty rules render as YAML
scenarios.then: valid YAML string returned, error is nil

scenarios.name: Empty map produces empty YAML
scenarios.then: empty or minimal YAML returned, error is nil

scenarios.name: Nil map produces empty YAML
scenarios.then: empty or minimal YAML returned, error is nil

requirements.description: MergePolicies SHALL merge inline rules over external rules with inline taking precedence
requirements.rationale: Architecture specifies precedence: policy-in-code < ConfigMap rules < inline rules

scenarios.name: Inline overrides external
scenarios.when: inline and external have overlapping keys
scenarios.then: inline value present in result

scenarios.name: Disjoint rules merged
scenarios.when: inline and external have no overlapping keys
scenarios.then: result contains all rules from both

scenarios.name: Nil inline returns copy of external
scenarios.then: result equals external

scenarios.name: Nil external returns copy of inline
scenarios.then: result equals inline

requirements.description: ValidatePolicyRules SHALL return field.ErrorList for empty keys or empty values
requirements.rationale: Invalid rules must be caught before rendering to prevent oslo.policy startup failures

scenarios.name: Valid rules pass
scenarios.when: all keys and values are non-empty
scenarios.then: empty field.ErrorList returned

scenarios.name: Empty key errors
scenarios.when: rule map has empty string key
scenarios.then: field.ErrorList has error for empty key

scenarios.name: Empty value errors
scenarios.when: rule map has key with empty value
scenarios.then: field.ErrorList has error for empty value

scenarios.name: Nil map passes
scenarios.then: empty field.ErrorList returned

requirements.description: All new packages SHALL have doc.go with SPDX license headers and Feature: CC-0004 tracking
requirements.rationale: Project convention from CC-0001/CC-0002 requires consistent file headers

scenarios.name: All 5 packages have doc.go
scenarios.when: types, conditions, config, plugins, policy packages are inspected
scenarios.then: each has doc.go with SPDX header and Feature: CC-0004

scenarios.name: All .go files have license headers
scenarios.then: file starts with SPDX-FileCopyrightText and SPDX-License-Identifier

requirements.description: Unit tests SHALL follow project Gomega conventions: dot-import, NewGomegaWithT(t), table-driven with t.Run
requirements.rationale: Consistency with existing CC-0002 test patterns across builders, assertions, simulators

scenarios.name: Tests use gomega dot import
scenarios.then: they import gomega with: . "github.com/onsi/gomega"

scenarios.name: Tests use NewGomegaWithT
scenarios.then: they start with g := NewGomegaWithT(t)

scenarios.name: Multi-scenario functions use table-driven tests
scenarios.then: tests use subtests with t.Run and a test cases slice

tasks.title: Create types/ package with 9 shared struct definitions and doc.go (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-013)
tasks.description: Create internal/common/types/doc.go and internal/common/types/types.go. Define all 9 types: ImageSpec, DatabaseSpec (with ClusterRef *corev1.LocalObjectReference, Host, Port, Database, SecretRef SecretRefSpec), MessagingSpec (ClusterRef, Hosts, SecretRef), CacheSpec (ClusterRef, Backend, Servers), SecretRefSpec (Name, Key), PolicySpec (Rules map, ConfigMapRef), PluginSpec (Name, ConfigSection, Config map), MiddlewareSpec (Name, FilterFactory, Position PipelinePosition, Config map), PipelinePosition (After, Before strings). All fields have json tags. Godoc comments document mutual exclusivity on DatabaseSpec, MessagingSpec, CacheSpec. License headers and Feature: CC-0004 on all files.

tasks.title: Create conditions/ package with 4 functions and doc.go (REQ-003, REQ-013)
tasks.description: Create internal/common/conditions/doc.go and internal/common/conditions/conditions.go. Implement: SetCondition(conditions *[]metav1.Condition, condition metav1.Condition) delegating to meta.SetStatusCondition; GetCondition(conditions []metav1.Condition, conditionType string) *metav1.Condition delegating to meta.FindStatusCondition; IsReady(conditions []metav1.Condition) bool checking type=Ready, status=True; AllTrue(conditions []metav1.Condition, types ...string) bool checking all named types have status=True. Import k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta.

tasks.title: Create conditions/ package unit tests (REQ-003, REQ-014)
tasks.description: Create internal/common/conditions/conditions_test.go with table-driven tests for all 4 functions. Test cases: SetCondition adds new condition, updates existing, handles nil/empty initial slice; GetCondition finds existing type, returns nil for missing type; IsReady returns true/false for Ready=True/False/missing; AllTrue returns true when all types True, false when any False, false when any missing, true with empty types variadic. Use gomega dot-import, NewGomegaWithT(t), License header, Feature: CC-0004.

tasks.title: Create config/ package with RenderINI and MergeDefaults functions and doc.go (REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-013)
tasks.description: Create internal/common/config/doc.go and internal/common/config/config.go. Implement RenderINI(sections map[string]map[string]string) string — sort section names, sort keys within sections, render [section]\nkey = value format. Implement MergeDefaults(userConfig, defaults map[string]map[string]string) map[string]map[string]string — create new map, copy defaults, overlay user values. Handle nil inputs by treating as empty maps. Return new maps (never mutate inputs).

tasks.title: Add InjectSecrets and InjectOsloPolicyConfig to config/ package (REQ-006, REQ-007)
tasks.description: Add to internal/common/config/config.go: InjectSecrets(config map[string]map[string]string, secrets map[string]string) map[string]map[string]string — iterate over config values, replace occurrences of secret keys (as placeholder tokens like {{KEY}}) with secret values, return new map. InjectOsloPolicyConfig(config map[string]map[string]string, policyFilePath string) — if policyFilePath is non-empty, ensure oslo_policy section exists and set policy_file key; if empty, no-op. Mutates config in-place per the function signature.

tasks.title: Create config/ package unit tests for all 4 functions (REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-007, REQ-014)
tasks.description: Create internal/common/config/config_test.go with table-driven tests. RenderINI: sorted sections, sorted keys, empty map, deterministic output, single section, multiple sections with multiple keys. MergeDefaults: user overrides defaults, defaults fill gaps, nil user, nil defaults, both nil, disjoint sections. InjectSecrets: placeholder replacement, no placeholders, empty secrets, nil secrets. InjectOsloPolicyConfig: inject with path, no-op with empty path, preserve existing section keys. Use gomega dot-import, NewGomegaWithT(t), Feature: CC-0004.

tasks.title: Create plugins/ package with PipelineSpec type and RenderPastePipeline function (REQ-008, REQ-013)
tasks.description: Create internal/common/plugins/doc.go and internal/common/plugins/plugins.go. Define package-internal (unexported) pipelineSpec struct: Pipelines map[string][]string (pipeline name -> ordered filter list), Filters map[string]filterDef (filter name -> factory + config), Middleware []MiddlewareSpec (from types package). Define unexported filterDef with Factory string and Config map[string]string. Implement RenderPastePipeline(spec pipelineSpec) string — iterate pipelines, for each middleware find position (After/Before) and insert into the filter list, render [composite:pipeline_name] and [filter:name] sections. Import types package for MiddlewareSpec and PipelinePosition. Export a PipelineSpec type alias or constructor to allow tests to create specs.

tasks.title: Add RenderPluginConfig function to plugins/ package (REQ-009)
tasks.description: Add to internal/common/plugins/plugins.go: RenderPluginConfig(plugins []PluginSpec) map[string]map[string]string — import PluginSpec from types package, iterate plugins, create section map keyed by ConfigSection with Config values. Handle nil/empty input by returning empty map.

tasks.title: Create plugins/ package unit tests (REQ-008, REQ-009, REQ-014)
tasks.description: Create internal/common/plugins/plugins_test.go with table-driven tests. RenderPastePipeline: base pipeline without middleware, middleware with Position.After, middleware with Position.Before, multiple middleware, empty PipelineSpec, middleware at pipeline boundaries (first/last position). RenderPluginConfig: single plugin, multiple plugins, empty list, nil list, plugin with empty config map. Use gomega dot-import, NewGomegaWithT(t), Feature: CC-0004.

tasks.title: Create policy/ package with RenderPolicyYAML, MergePolicies, ValidatePolicyRules and doc.go (REQ-010, REQ-011, REQ-012, REQ-013)
tasks.description: Create internal/common/policy/doc.go and internal/common/policy/policy.go. Implement: RenderPolicyYAML(rules map[string]string) (string, error) — use sigs.k8s.io/yaml or gopkg.in/yaml.v3 to marshal rules to YAML, handle nil/empty by returning empty string. MergePolicies(inline, external map[string]string) map[string]string — create new map, copy external, overlay inline. ValidatePolicyRules(rules map[string]string) field.ErrorList — iterate rules, add field.Invalid for empty keys, add field.Invalid for empty values. Import k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation/field.

tasks.title: Create policy/ package unit tests (REQ-010, REQ-011, REQ-012, REQ-014)
tasks.description: Create internal/common/policy/policy_test.go with table-driven tests. RenderPolicyYAML: non-empty rules, empty map, nil map, single rule, multiple rules with output validation. MergePolicies: inline overrides external, disjoint merge, nil inline, nil external, both nil. ValidatePolicyRules: valid rules, empty key error, empty value error, multiple errors, nil map, empty map. Use gomega dot-import, NewGomegaWithT(t), Feature: CC-0004.

tasks.title: Verify go vet, golangci-lint, and test coverage pass for all new packages (REQ-001, REQ-013, REQ-014)
tasks.description: Run 'cd internal/common && go vet ./types/... ./conditions/... ./config/... ./plugins/... ./policy/...' and 'cd internal/common && golangci-lint run ./types/... ./conditions/... ./config/... ./plugins/... ./policy/...' and 'cd internal/common && go test -cover ./conditions/... ./config/... ./plugins/... ./policy/...' to verify everything compiles, passes lint, and achieves 80%+ coverage. Fix any issues found.

test_specifications.story: Operator developer manages status conditions programmatically
test_specifications.expected: Calling SetCondition with a new type should add the condition to the slice

test_specifications.story: Operator developer manages status conditions programmatically
test_specifications.expected: Calling SetCondition with an existing type should update it in-place

test_specifications.story: Operator developer manages status conditions programmatically
test_specifications.expected: GetCondition should return nil when the type is not in the slice

test_specifications.story: Operator developer manages status conditions programmatically
test_specifications.expected: IsReady should return true when Ready condition exists with status True

test_specifications.story: Operator developer manages status conditions programmatically
test_specifications.expected: IsReady should return false when no Ready condition exists

test_specifications.story: Operator developer manages status conditions programmatically
test_specifications.expected: AllTrue should return false when a requested type is not in the slice

test_specifications.story: Operator developer manages status conditions programmatically
test_specifications.expected: AllTrue should return true when all requested types have status True

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders INI config files from Go maps
test_specifications.expected: RenderINI should output sections in alphabetical order

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders INI config files from Go maps
test_specifications.expected: RenderINI should sort keys alphabetically within each section

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders INI config files from Go maps
test_specifications.expected: RenderINI with empty map should return empty string

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders INI config files from Go maps
test_specifications.expected: Two calls with same input should produce identical output

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders INI config files from Go maps
test_specifications.expected: User values should take precedence over defaults for same key

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders INI config files from Go maps
test_specifications.expected: Default values for keys not in user config should appear in result

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders INI config files from Go maps
test_specifications.expected: Nil user config returns defaults; nil defaults returns user config; both nil returns empty

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders INI config files from Go maps
test_specifications.expected: Placeholder tokens matching secret keys should be replaced with values

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders INI config files from Go maps
test_specifications.expected: Empty secrets map should return config unchanged

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders INI config files from Go maps
test_specifications.expected: Non-empty policyFilePath should add oslo_policy section with policy_file key

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders INI config files from Go maps
test_specifications.expected: Empty policyFilePath should not modify config

test_specifications.story: Operator developer generates PasteDeploy pipeline configs
test_specifications.expected: PipelineSpec with no middleware should render only base pipeline sections

test_specifications.story: Operator developer generates PasteDeploy pipeline configs
test_specifications.expected: Middleware with Position.After should appear after the named filter in pipeline

test_specifications.story: Operator developer generates PasteDeploy pipeline configs
test_specifications.expected: Middleware with Position.Before should appear before the named filter in pipeline

test_specifications.story: Operator developer generates PasteDeploy pipeline configs
test_specifications.expected: Empty PipelineSpec should return empty string

test_specifications.story: Operator developer generates PasteDeploy pipeline configs
test_specifications.expected: Single PluginSpec should produce one section with correct key-values

test_specifications.story: Operator developer generates PasteDeploy pipeline configs
test_specifications.expected: Empty/nil plugin list should return empty map

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders and validates oslo.policy YAML files
test_specifications.expected: Non-empty rule map should produce valid YAML string with no error

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders and validates oslo.policy YAML files
test_specifications.expected: Empty/nil rule map should produce empty YAML with no error

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders and validates oslo.policy YAML files
test_specifications.expected: Inline rules should override external rules for overlapping keys

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders and validates oslo.policy YAML files
test_specifications.expected: Disjoint rule sets should be fully merged into result

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders and validates oslo.policy YAML files
test_specifications.expected: Nil inline returns external copy; nil external returns inline copy

test_specifications.expected: Valid rules should return empty field.ErrorList

test_specifications.expected: Empty key should produce field error in ErrorList

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders and validates oslo.policy YAML files
test_specifications.expected: Empty value should produce field error in ErrorList

test_specifications.story: Operator developer renders and validates oslo.policy YAML files
test_specifications.expected: Nil map should return empty field.ErrorList

review_criteria: All 9 types compile with correct JSON tags, go vet passes, and fields match architecture doc specifications
All 13 pure functions are implemented with correct signatures matching the elaborated description
All unit tests pass with 80%+ coverage for conditions/, config/, plugins/, and policy/ packages
Test files use project Gomega conventions: dot-import, NewGomegaWithT(t), table-driven where appropriate
All files have SPDX Apache-2.0 license headers and Feature: CC-0004 tracking comments
doc.go exists for each of the 5 new packages (types, conditions, config, plugins, policy)
golangci-lint passes on all new code without errors
No Kubernetes API calls in any function — all functions are pure (conditions wraps meta helpers only, no client calls)
MergeDefaults and MergePolicies return new maps without mutating inputs; InjectOsloPolicyConfig mutates in-place as documented
RenderINI output is deterministic (sorted sections, sorted keys) verified by test
implementation_notes:
This feature implements the pure-function foundation of internal/common/ (S004 from the architecture plan). Key architectural decisions:

1. **No Validate() methods on types** — Validation is deferred to CRD CEL markers (S011). Types only carry doc comments about constraints.

2. **Types in a single file** — All 9 types are defined in types/types.go since they are small structs. This keeps imports clean for consumers.

3. **Conditions wrap meta helpers** — SetCondition/GetCondition delegate to k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta.SetStatusCondition and meta.FindStatusCondition. These are not K8s API calls; they operate on in-memory slices.

4. **Config functions operate on map[string]map[string]string** — This maps directly to INI [section]/key=value structure. RenderINI sorts to ensure deterministic output for content-hash-based ConfigMap naming.

5. **InjectSecrets placeholder format** — Uses {{KEY}} tokens in config values that are replaced with secret values. This matches the connection string assembly pattern from the architecture docs.

6. **PipelineSpec is package-internal** — The plugins/ package exports functions but keeps PipelineSpec unexported to prevent external packages from constructing invalid specs. A NewPipelineSpec constructor or similar is exposed for tests.

7. **YAML serialization for policy** — Uses sigs.k8s.io/yaml (already a transitive dependency via controller-runtime) for oslo.policy YAML rendering.

8. **field.ErrorList for validation** — ValidatePolicyRules returns k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation/field.ErrorList, the standard K8s validation error type. This enables webhook integration in S011.

**Key files and patterns to follow:**
- License headers: See internal/common/bootstrap/doc.go for format
- Test conventions: See internal/common/testutil/builders/secret_builder_test.go for Gomega pattern
- Feature tracking: All files include `// Feature: CC-0004` comment
- Module: All code belongs to the github.com/c5c3/forge/internal/common module (go.mod already exists)

**Risks:**
- The plugins/ PipelineSpec design must accommodate future OpenStack services beyond Keystone without being over-engineered now. Keep the internal type minimal.
- InjectSecrets placeholder format ({{KEY}}) must match what reconcilers in S005/S013 will use. Document the format clearly in godoc.
